description:
You are helping a biomedical researcher tidy up her dataset directory so that the analysis scripts always look in the same place for the “train” and “test” CSV files.

Current situation  
/home/user/data/original/ already contains the two real data files:  
• /home/user/data/original/train_set.csv  
• /home/user/data/original/test_set.csv  

A separate working directory already exists but is empty:  
/home/user/data/working/  

Goal  
1. Inside /home/user/data/working/ create **symbolic links** (not copies) named  
   • train.csv → /home/user/data/original/train_set.csv  
   • test.csv  → /home/user/data/original/test_set.csv  
   Make sure you use absolute paths so that the links remain valid even if the current directory changes.

2. After the links are in place, generate a very small text report that lists the links and their targets.  
   • File path for the report: /home/user/data/symlink_verification.log  
   • Exact required format (two lines, no extra whitespace or trailing slash):  
     train.csv -> /home/user/data/original/train_set.csv  
     test.csv  -> /home/user/data/original/test_set.csv  

     The arrow token must be literally space–hyphen–greater-than (“ -> ”) with single spaces on both sides, exactly as shown.

Constraints & notes  
• Accomplish all of this with only two or three standard Linux commands.  
• Overwrite /home/user/data/symlink_verification.log if it already exists.  
• Do NOT move or copy the original CSV files; only create links.  
• When you finish, the automated checker will:  
  a. Confirm that train.csv and test.csv exist in /home/user/data/working/ and are symbolic links to the correct absolute targets.  
  b. Read /home/user/data/symlink_verification.log and verify the two-line format exactly.

No further output is required from you once the commands complete successfully.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before agent starts)
----------------------------------------
Directory /home/user/data/original/ exists and contains two regular files:
1) /home/user/data/original/train_set.csv
   Content (first few bytes, not relevant to test): “id,value,…”
2) /home/user/data/original/test_set.csv
   Content: “id,value,…”

Directory /home/user/data/working/ exists and is empty.

No file /home/user/data/symlink_verification.log is present.

Expected final state (after agent’s work)
-----------------------------------------
1) /home/user/data/working/train.csv
   • Type: symbolic link
   • Link target (absolute): /home/user/data/original/train_set.csv

2) /home/user/data/working/test.csv
   • Type: symbolic link
   • Link target (absolute): /home/user/data/original/test_set.csv

3) /home/user/data/symlink_verification.log
   • Type: regular text file
   • Exact contents (including new-line at end of each line, UNIX LF):
     train.csv -> /home/user/data/original/train_set.csv\n
     test.csv  -> /home/user/data/original/test_set.csv\n
